meta.title: UK Community Pharmacy Openings and Closures Index
meta.description: Verified data on England's community pharmacy network: NHS dispensing-contractor counts from NHSBSA open data and Companies House SIC 47730 corporate-churn data. Two independent official spines. Updated quarterly on NHSBSA publication.
meta.licence: Open Government Licence v3.0 (OGL3). Derived from NHSBSA open data and Companies House public register. Reuse with attribution.
meta.citeAs: UK Community Pharmacy Openings and Closures Index, derived from NHSBSA Pharmacy and Appliance Contractor Dispensing Data and Companies House Advanced Search API. Published under OGL3. Verified 2026-07-14.
meta.methodology: NHS dispensing-contractor counts: unique CONTRACTOR_CODE values with PHARMACY_ACCOUNT_TYPE='Pharmacy' in each monthly NHSBSA dispensing-contractors CSV (March snapshots used as the annual benchmark month to avoid seasonal noise). Appliance contractors are counted separately and excluded from the pharmacy total. Source: NHSBSA Pharmacy and Appliance Contractor Dispensing Data, opendata.nhsbsa.net. Companies House SIC 47730 corporate counts: live active-company and dissolved-company hit counts from the CH Advanced Search API (sic_codes=47730), pulled 2026-07-14. CH counts include incorporated companies only; sole traders and partnership pharmacies are not visible in CH data. Dissolution date is the formal strike-off date, which typically lags trading closure by several months.
caveats: NHSBSA dispensing-contractor counts cover England only. Scotland uses PSD (Public Services Scotland) data; Wales uses NWSSP; Scotland and Wales v2 are queued.
NHSBSA counts include only pharmacies with active NHS dispensing contracts. Wholly private pharmacies without an NHS contract are not included.
Companies House SIC 47730 figures are UK-wide (not England-only) and include companies that may not hold NHS contracts or may be holding companies for pharmacy groups.
SIC codes are self-reported; occasional miscoding means SIC 47730 may exclude some pharmacy companies or include non-pharmacy retail health businesses.
March snapshots are used as the annual reference point. Intra-year data may show seasonal variation.

A contractor that submits no prescriptions in a given month does not appear in that month's NHSBSA CSV; the March count therefore reflects pharmacies that dispensed at least one prescription in March.

nhsbsa_dispensing.name: NHSBSA Pharmacy and Appliance Contractor Dispensing Data

nhsbsaContractors.description: England NHS dispensing-pharmacy contractor counts (March snapshots, NHSBSA open data). Appliance contractors excluded.
nhsbsaContractors.note: Counts unique CONTRACTOR_CODE values with PHARMACY_ACCOUNT_TYPE='Pharmacy' in each March monthly file.

companiesHouseSIC47730.description: Companies House SIC 47730 ('Dispensing chemist in specialised stores'), UK-wide incorporated companies. Active and dissolved counts from CH Advanced Search API, pulled 2026-07-14.
companiesHouseSIC47730.note: Active count includes all company statuses returned as 'active' by CH. Dissolved count uses company_status=dissolved filter. Both are UK-wide, not England-only.

quarterlyChurn.description: Quarterly incorporations and dissolutions for SIC 47730 by CH incorporated_from/dissolved_from date ranges. Pulled 2026-07-14.
quarterlyChurn.caveat: CH incorporations/dissolutions measure corporate-entity churn. A new incorporation may be a group restrucuring, a new entrant, or a change of legal form; it does not always correspond to a new pharmacy opening. Similarly, dissolution may lag trading closure by many months. CH data is complementary to NHSBSA contractor counts, not a substitute.

networkContext.scotlandWalesNote: Scotland dispensing data is published by Public Services Scotland (PSD); Wales by NHS Wales Shared Services Partnership (NWSSP). These devolved datasets are not included in v1 of this index. UK-wide comparison will be added in v2.
networkContext.fundingContext: England's community pharmacy network is funded primarily under the Community Pharmacy Contractual Framework (CPCF). The NHSBSA contractor count is the most granular public signal of network size: a contractor that disappears from the monthly dispensing register has either closed, surrendered its NHS contract, or merged into another pharmacy. The 1,382-contractor decline since March 2019 reflects the sustained funding and margin pressure that has driven trade press coverage of the 'pharmacy closures crisis'.
networkContext.ownershipNote: NHSBSA dispensing data does not distinguish independent pharmacies from multiples. The Companies House data, filtered to SIC 47730, captures the corporate layer but cannot cleanly separate independent single-site operators from holding companies and subsidiaries of multiples. Ownership-split analysis requires cross-referencing NHSBSA contractor names against CH group structures, which is the planned v2 enhancement.
